message:
feat(export): add --clipboard / -c flag to cass export (#199)

Closes #199 — outside-contributor request for a one-keystroke "thread
into clipboard" path that pairs with the existing `cass resume`.

`cass resume <path>` already covers the "open this thread back inside
the original coding agent" half of the request for the harnesses where
that's mechanically possible (claude / codex / opencode / pi / gemini).
Cursor doesn't expose a session-resumption surface, so the reporter
explicitly asked for the fallback: pull the thread, copy it to the
clipboard, paste into a new chat.

This patch makes that fallback first-class:

  cass export <path> --clipboard
  cass export <path> -c --format=markdown

Behaviour:
- Renders the session in the chosen format (Markdown / Text / JSON /
  HTML — same path as today) and streams it into the platform's
  clipboard tool: pbcopy on macOS, clip on Windows, wl-copy → xclip →
  xsel on Linux/BSD (Wayland-first because X11 selections aren't
  visible to Wayland clients).
- Mutually exclusive with `--output`: `clipboard` and a file path are
  alternative destinations, conflicts_with on the clap arg surfaces
  that as a parse error.
- On clipboard success: prints `Copied N bytes to clipboard via <tool>.
  Paste into your coding agent's chat to resume the conversation.` to
  stderr so stdout stays empty and the export isn't doubled.
- On clipboard failure (headless / SSH / no tool on PATH): warns on
  stderr and falls back to printing the export to stdout, so a wrapper
  script piping `cass export -c | something` still works and the user
  doesn't lose the conversation.

Implementation choice: shells out to the platform tool rather than
adding an `arboard`/`copypasta` dependency. The shell-out path covers
all four targets the project ships to today, fails predictably when no
tool is installed (which is the right signal for SSH sessions), and
keeps the dep tree where it is. The helper iterates candidates in
order, captures the last spawn/exit error, and surfaces a single
"tried X, Y, Z; last error: …" message so debugging on weird hosts is
straightforward.

Verification:
- `cass export --help` lists `-c, --clipboard` with the documented
  semantics.
- New unit test (`clipboard_helper_tests::returns_err_when_no_tool_is_available`)
  pins the headless-fallback contract by clearing PATH and asserting
  the helper returns Err with a "tried" listing.
- `cargo test --lib`: 4127 passed / 0 failed.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
